plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core physician traits directly map to innovation leadership traits, reframing clinicians as already equipped to innovate.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as someone else's domain to recognizing clinicians as natural innovators through trait-mapping between physicianship and innovation leadership
plan_units.rationale: This is the conceptual opener for Part 3 and establishes the module’s core identity shift: physicians do not need a new personality to innovate; they need to redeploy existing clinical strengths toward system problems.
source_refs: You do not need to learn
plan_units.title: Physicianship Equals Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Map the clinical method to design thinking so learners can understand innovation as an extension of routine diagnostic reasoning.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From abstract innovation jargon to familiar clinical reasoning through direct mapping of design thinking onto the clinical method
plan_units.rationale: This comparison is a diagram-driven, simultaneous whole whose value comes from seeing the clinical and innovation workflows side by side. It is central and should remain dense rather than decomposed.
source_refs: changing the 'patient' from a human being
to a broken workflow or system
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis Equals Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable formula for driving change from within an organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From vague notions of internal innovation to a structured model of intrapreneurship through guided reading and formula framing
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly requires a reading block before Slide 3 and frames it as foundational for understanding structured intrapreneurship and stakeholder alignment.
source_refs: focus on how Lam breaks down intrapreneurship
a calculated approach to driving change from within
plan_units.title: Required Reading: The Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that intrapreneurship depends on balancing clinical vision with organizational realities, leveraging existing resources, and aligning stakeholders.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From vague notions of internal innovation to a structured model of intrapreneurship through guided reading and formula framing
plan_units.rationale: This is the key explanatory expansion of the reading and deserves a single interstitial because the idea has high pedagogical weight but can be clarified in one focused beat.
source_refs: master the art of internal stakeholder alignment
skill we will heavily focus on
plan_units.title: Lam’s Formula: Vision Within Organizational Reality

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and locate the clinician’s most immediate arena for innovation within existing health systems.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup creation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through contrast, constraint, and institutional framing
plan_units.rationale: This is a major conceptual distinction with multiple explanatory beats and very high pedagogical weight. It benefits from a full cluster that moves from definition to complication to institutional navigation.
source_refs: improve it from the inside out
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand that innovation is often wrongly equated with leaving the hospital, when internal change may offer the most immediate impact.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup creation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through contrast, constraint, and institutional framing
plan_units.rationale: This tension beat surfaces the false default assumption that innovation means entrepreneurship, setting up the need for the intrapreneurial frame.
source_refs: We often conflate innovation with Entrepreneurship
start a new company from scratch
most immediate impact you can make
plan_units.title: The Arena Shift: Startup Myth vs Internal Impact

plan_units.learning_objective: Visualize why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because innovation must occur within load-bearing institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup creation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through contrast, constraint, and institutional framing
plan_units.rationale: The design brief is a high-value visual reframe and fits the develop beat by changing the learner’s frame of reference from blank-canvas building to constrained redesign.
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur’s Maze

plan_units.learning_objective: Conclude that successful intrapreneurs must work with legacy systems, compliance, politics, and budgets rather than ignoring them.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup creation to understanding intrapreneurship as internal system change through contrast, constraint, and institutional framing
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat lands the practical takeaway and aligns closely with the course promise described in the speaker notes.
source_refs: legacy IT systems, compliance rules, and operational budgets
you will bring the roof down
redesign the room without breaking the building
plan_units.title: Redesign the Room Without Breaking the Building

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish a raw idea from a rigorously vetted opportunity with feasibility, viability, desirability, and a path to execution.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first thinking to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, deconstruction, and worked problem framing
plan_units.rationale: This is one of the most important threshold concepts in the lesson and directly underpins later application. It needs a full cluster to move from definition to disciplined reframing.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain first principles thinking as a method for deconstructing inherited workflows into fundamental truths before rebuilding them.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first thinking to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, deconstruction, and worked problem framing
plan_units.rationale: This beat deepens the opportunity concept by showing the analytical discipline required to move beyond superficial iteration.
source_refs: Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: First Principles: Break the Broken System Down

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing by contrasting a superficial solution idea with a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first thinking to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, deconstruction, and worked problem framing
plan_units.rationale: This is the concrete example that makes the abstract distinction usable. It belongs in the develop beat as an exemplifying case.
source_refs: design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
plan_units.title: Worked Example: Bad Idea to Good Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Conclude that the program’s goal is not idea generation but actionable opportunity development inside a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first thinking to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, deconstruction, and worked problem framing
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat crystallizes the operational posture learners are meant to adopt going forward.
plan_units.title: From Generating Ideas to Engineering Opportunities

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the module’s three takeaways: clinicians are already equipped to innovate, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities matter more than ideas.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From discrete concepts to a coherent intrapreneurial stance through recap and transition
plan_units.rationale: This is a classic synthesis slide whose value lies in simultaneous recap and forward transition, so it should remain a dense singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify recall and understanding of core distinctions: idea vs opportunity, clinical method vs design thinking, intrapreneurship, and problem-first innovation posture.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verified understanding through targeted recall and concept discrimination
plan_units.rationale: The source includes a dedicated knowledge check with explicit answers and rationales, making it a required assessment-aligned unit.
source_refs: rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
fall in love with the problem

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner needs the primary-source basis and correct workbook reproduction guidance for Sandra Lam’s Chapter 4 formula and venn diagram reference.
Learner needs institutional examples of internal constraints to make the intrapreneurship blueprint metaphor concrete in workbook notes.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded explanation of each overlapping trait—empathy, curiosity, analytical prowess, resiliency, and comfort with ambiguity—and how each already appears in clinical work and transfers to system innovation.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The fast visual identity shift that physicianship and innovation leadership overlap substantially.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain how one clinical trait transfers to innovation work.

sections.narrative_intent: Support the mindset reframe with enough reflective depth that clinicians internalize innovation as an extension of who they already are.
sections.title: Trait Translation: Why Physicians Already Belong in Innovation

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Step-by-step unpacking of how history-taking maps to empathize, diagnosis maps to define, and treatment planning maps to ideate/prototype, with room for written examples.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The side-by-side visual mapping between the two workflows.

exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to translate a familiar clinical encounter into design-thinking language.

sections.narrative_intent: Convert abstraction into recognizable practice so learners feel procedural familiarity rather than jargon resistance.
sections.title: Clinical Method as Design Thinking

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Longer explanation of why internal innovation is constrained by hospital politics, compliance, budgets, and legacy systems, plus a reflective comparison of entrepreneur vs intrapreneur risk and leverage.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The contrast definition and blueprint metaphor.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners classify obstacles in their own setting as load-bearing constraints rather than annoyances.

sections.narrative_intent: Deepen institutional realism so learners begin seeing stakeholder alignment and constraint navigation as part of the innovation craft.
sections.title: Working Inside the Building: The Logic of Intrapreneurship

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Detailed written practice on feasibility, viability, desirability, root-cause framing, first-principles decomposition, and rewriting weak solution statements into opportunity statements.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The high-level distinction between idea and opportunity and the worked-example contrast.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners diagnose why a proposed innovation statement is solution-biased.
Have learners draft an opportunity statement rooted in a chief complaint and root cause.

sections.narrative_intent: Carry the cognitive depth that cannot fit on slides: the disciplined move from complaint or gadget preference to an actionable organizational opportunity.
sections.title: From Idea to Opportunity: Problem Framing Workbook

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Space for learners to justify why distractors are wrong and explain correct answers in their own words.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: fall in love with the problem
exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to explain the rationale behind one correct answer and one rejected distractor.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn recognition into explanation so learners consolidate terminology and conceptual boundaries before moving to the next module.
sections.title: Knowledge Check Review and Self-Explanation

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: Part 3 reframes clinicians as natural innovators, bridges clinical diagnosis to design thinking, distinguishes intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship, and teaches the critical shift from solution-first ideas to rigorously framed organizational opportunities. The plan uses clustering only where concept density and pedagogical weight warrant progressive disclosure, while preserving key comparison, synthesis, and diagram-led moments as keep-dense singletons.

plan_units.rationale: This is the conceptual opener for Part 3 and establishes the module’s core identity shift: physicians do not need a new personality to innovate; they need to redeploy existing clinical strengths toward system problems.

plan_units.rationale: This beat deepens the opportunity concept by showing the analytical discipline required to move beyond superficial iteration.
source_refs: Building a new system from the ground up
plan_units.title: First Principles: Break the Broken System Down

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing by contrasting a superficial solution idea with a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first thinking to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, deconstruction, and worked problem framing
plan_units.rationale: This is the concrete example that makes the abstract distinction usable. It belongs in the develop beat as an exemplifying case.
source_refs: design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
plan_units.title: Worked Example: Bad Idea to Good Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Conclude that the program’s goal is not idea generation but actionable opportunity development inside a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first thinking to disciplined opportunity engineering through definition, deconstruction, and worked problem framing
plan_units.rationale: This resolve beat crystallizes the operational posture learners are meant to adopt going forward.
plan_units.title: From Generating Ideas to Engineering Opportunities

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the module’s three takeaways: clinicians are already equipped to innovate, intrapreneurship is the arena, and opportunities matter more than ideas.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From discrete concepts to a coherent intrapreneurial stance through recap and transition
plan_units.rationale: This is a classic synthesis slide whose value lies in simultaneous recap and forward transition, so it should remain a dense singleton.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify recall and understanding of core distinctions: idea vs opportunity, clinical method vs design thinking, intrapreneurship, and problem-first innovation posture.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verified understanding through targeted recall and concept discrimination
plan_units.rationale: The source includes a dedicated knowledge check with explicit answers and rationales, making it a required assessment-aligned unit.
